Petition To Compel Trustee To Pay Creditors Claim Forthwith Or To Show Cause Why He Should Not Be Held In Contempt; In The Event Of Nonpayment To Void The Transfer Of 1074 Treat Ave And Return It To The Trust, To Appoint A Receiver, And To Order That The Property Be Sold To Satisfy Petitioners Claim; And For An Order That The Trustee Be Enjoined From Further Encumbering The Treat Ave Property Until This Matter Is Resolved; And For Attorneys Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Feb-04-2019 Continued To Mar-22-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lynn Searle for RJ Cohen, Inc. (Petitioner).
Michael McCloskey for Valentino Fazzari (Respondent).

The Court asks how this matter is going to be resolved. Counsel expressed their positions with respect to the property being transferred back into the estate, the Court voiding out the transfer of the property into the trust, Mr. Fazzari coming up with the money and paying the debt, RJ Cohen not filing a civil law suit and not acting timely, etc. Ms. Searle stated that she will not do mediation. The Petition to Compel Trustee to Pay Claim, etc. is continued to March 22, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for an evidentiary hearing. Witness lists, exhibit lists and trial briefs are to be filed by March 15, 2019.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Reporter: Nancy Presant, CSR 906, 510-757-8550, npresantcsr@gmail.com
